gene_region.label: CFTR transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical CFTR genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: CFTR encodes an ATP-binding cassette chloride and bicarbonate channel that regulates epithelial salt and water transport in lung, pancreas, intestine, sweat gland, and reproductive tissues.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local CFTR bundle is cystic-fibrosis and epithelial-ion-transport oriented. CFTR interpretation requires exact variant class, phase, zygosity, phenotype, and external clinical classification.
variant_effect_overview: CFTR pathogenic variants can affect channel synthesis, folding, gating, conductance, splicing, or protein stability.
Carrier, compound-heterozygous, and modifier contexts should be kept separate from single-site exploratory findings.
condition_research_overview: Cystic fibrosis and CFTR-related disorder context
Airway mucus hydration and pancreatic/intestinal biology
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: CFTR methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for CFTR.
Because CFTR biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use CFTR promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking.
Cystic fibrosis and CFTR-related disorder context

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000001626: CFTR coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: CFTR biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking
recommended_icons: ATP sensitive potassium channel (open, in membrane)
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a CFTR epithelial-ion-channel thesis: the individual may carry a marker relevant to chloride/bicarbonate transport, channel folding or gating, mucus hydration, cystic-fibrosis biology, or CFTR-related disorder review. Clinical interpretation requires phase, zygosity, exact allele classification, and phenotype match.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: CFTR p.Phe508del / F508del is curated as a CFTR marker for Cystic fibrosis and CFTR-related disorder context, Epithelial chloride/bicarbonate transport. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Cystic fibrosis and CFTR-related disorder context
Airway mucus hydration and pancreatic/intestinal biology
research_context: Use this marker as a CFTR pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair CFTR p.Phe508del / F508del with CFTR methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: CFTR p.Phe508del / F508del or linked CFTR locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Cystic fibrosis and CFTR-related disorder context, Epithelial chloride/bicarbonate transport
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats CFTR p.Phe508del / F508del as a gene-specific marker for Cystic fibrosis and CFTR-related disorder context, Epithelial chloride/bicarbonate transport and ATP-regulated chloride and bicarbonate channel function, epithelial fluid balance, mucus hydration, and ABC transporter folding/trafficking.
